parsed_review.summary: CP00-374 is a descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P03.M06.S13-RP12.P04.M03.S20, 150 units) that closes RP12.P03 and opens the RP12.P04 foundation as nine descriptor-only sections. The pack-level claim is honestly production_ready=false / status=implemented_pending_review, supported by implementation (service.js/registry.js), tests (3 dedicated CP374 tests in model.test.js), the contract projection, and the RP12 validator — all of which enforce descriptor-only, no-write (writes_product_state=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), and no-real-data (no_real_data=true, synthetic_only=true) boundaries. Unit count, deliverable distribution, and micro-title distribution match exactly across the prompt baseline, registry requirements, manifest, and validate script (sum=150). Claude is correctly held as a read-only, non-final-approver reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob), and Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors, never product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, 0-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; readiness auth preflight passed with non-zero stdout; prompt is within the 25600B limit; forbidden tools absent; summary-only correctly prohibited for Risk C. Changed-file scope is clean — all dirty files are within active implementation refs and unrelated dirty files are correctly excluded. One non-blocking P3 readability observation; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Does not block pack or goal closeout; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust or runtime opening.

findings.title: Cumulative no_write_attestation retains CP364-era label under CP00-374 pack_id
findings.details: BILLING_CORE_CP374_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is built by spreading the CP373 attestation (which transitively spreads down to the CP364 base) and overriding pack_id to CP00-374 plus adding validates_p03_closeout_p04_foundation_descriptor_only:true. As a result the CP374 attestation (and the mirrored contracts/billing-core-contract.json top-level no_write_attestation at lines 2211-2241) still carries the original CP364 field validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true under pack_id CP00-374. This is intentional cumulative attestation and is strictly drift-guarded by the validator's assert.deepEqual against the registry constant, so there is no correctness or boundary risk; it is purely a readability nuance for a future reader scanning the contract who may momentarily expect a CP364-named field not to appear under a CP374 pack_id.
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout. Optionally, a future cleanup could add a short comment documenting the cumulative-attestation spread chain, or rename the accumulated descriptor-validation flags to a single list, to make the intent self-evident.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as by-design cumulative attestation; non-blocking; no change required for CP00-374 closeout.
